summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a classic ’80 Nassau show, highlighted by an electrifying Althea and stellar China > Rider sequence.
review: The recording is a well‑balanced Clugston soundboard mix that sounds clear overall, though a few tracks benefit from slight pitch correction. Musically the concert shines with Jerry’s soaring lead on Althea, a mind‑blowing second solo in China > Rider, and powerful performances of Eyes of the World and Morning Dew; only a couple of over‑played Go To Heaven cuts and a flat‑sounding Ramble On Rose detract. Fans consistently praise the energy and cohesion of both sets.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews rate the show 4‑5 stars, praising key performances and noting only minor recording quirks; the weighted average yields a solid four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Decent mix with clear instrument separation; some reviewers note slight pitch issues on certain tracks (e.g., Ramble On Rose) and occasional flatness, but overall the SBD is clean and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds energy with high‑time jams and a strong China > Rider finish; the second set maintains momentum, delivering an especially hot Althea and a soaring Morning Dew, despite a few over‑played Go To Heaven cuts.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring lead tones on Althea, killer solos in China > Rider, and overall great guitar work; one reviewer notes a broken string during Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (organ) highlighted for killing it on Truckin'; keyboards generally described as solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey stays for Space and provides steady, energetic drumming; overall drum work noted as strong.
